exemplar.title: Can New Knowledge Change Establish Values Or Beliefs?

detailedExemplarData.title: Can New Knowledge Change Establish Values Or Beliefs?

strengths: Clear identification of three distinct objects with real-world contexts (personal LAM shirt, The 100 novel series, Seaspiracy documentary)
Meaningful engagement with personal experience (childhood faith journey) demonstrating awareness of knowledgeâs role in belief change
Recognition of multiple perspectives (family reactions to Seaspiracy) showing TOK insight
Consistent attempt to link each object to the chosen IA prompt
weaknesses: Explanations remain largely descriptive and lack analytical depth (TOK concepts such as ways of knowing are not fully explored)
Supporting evidence is thin or anecdotal; key assertions are unsupported by external references
Connections between objects and prompt are uneven in clarity and precision
Structure and coherence suffer from incomplete thoughts, repetition, and occasional grammar errors
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The official examinerâs total mark of 8 places this exhibition in the 7â8 band, which requires clear identification of three objects, well-explained links to the IA prompt, and strong justification supported by evidence. While the student successfully names three concrete objects and provides real-world contexts, and shows genuine engagement with the prompt, the analysis often remains at a descriptive level and lacks deeper TOK exploration. Despite these weaknesses, the overall qualityâespecially the consistent linking of objects to the prompt and the awareness of multiple perspectivesâwarrants the high score of 8, reflecting substantial achievement in meeting the band descriptors.

annotations.comment: The overview of documentary content is descriptive but lacks evaluation of evidence quality. Critically assess the reliability of the statistics and interviews.
The introduction does not explicitly state the chosen IA prompt or its connection to the objects. Clarify which prompt is being addressed and frame the exhibition around it.
Key TOK terminology (e.g., âways of knowing,â âareas of knowledgeâ) is missing here. Integrate these terms to demonstrate familiarity with the TOK framework.
The student clearly identifies the personal LAM shirt as an object linked to belief change, showing engagement with personal experience.
The narrative of belief change is descriptive but lacks analysis of how the shirt itself embodies the TOK prompt. Explain how the object functions as a vehicle of knowledge.
Personal anecdote is used without supporting evidence or external references. Incorporate examples or research to substantiate claims about the religious group.
The statement âWhen we are born, we are born with no knowledgeâ is asserted without reference to philosophical theories. Consider discussing Lockeâs tabula rasa or alternative perspectives.
Linking personal religious change to broader TOK frameworks would strengthen the analysis. Reference specific knowledge frameworks or methods of inquiry.
Introducing âThe 100â as the second object provides a fictional lens, but its TOK relevance needs clearer context. Describe why this story was chosen over real-world cases.
The discussion of survival-driven belief change is interesting but remains implied. Expand on how new information in the narrative concretely challenges established values.
The sentence ending âWe can connect this to scientificâ is incomplete and interrupts coherence. Ensure each thought is fully developed.
Throughout the shirt discussion, counterclaims are omitted. Address potential objections to your interpretation to deepen analysis.
The personal reflection on belief change repeats points from earlier paragraphs. Strive for fresh insights or examples in each section.
Extract specific scenes or passages from the novel to anchor your claims about belief and survival, rather than speaking in generalities.
Consider how the fictional context of The 100 differs from real-world knowledge acquisition, and address any limitations of this analogy.
Naming âSeaspiracyâ as the third object with class context provides clear real-world linkage, demonstrating contextual awareness.
The student notes differing family reactions, showing awareness of multiple perspectivesâa valuable TOK insight.
Although perspectives are noted, the link between family disagreement and the central prompt is not articulated. Make this connection explicit.
The conclusion on page 2 notes that knowledge does not always change beliefs but stops short of exploring why. Examine cognitive biases or emotional factors.
The reference to natural and human sciences is a start, but the student does not discuss specific ways of knowing or the scope and limitations of science.
Please provide concrete examples of scientific theories (e.g., evolution of atomic model) to illustrate how new knowledge overturns established beliefs.
Spelling and grammar errors (e.g., âty showâ) appear in the The 100 section and can distract from the argument. Proofread carefully.
The student could strengthen coherence by formulating a single knowledge question to guide analysis across all three objects.
Overall connections between objects remain fragmented. Create a comparative framework to show how each object uniquely illustrates knowledge-driven belief change.
Include precise details about the documentaryâs release date, director, or source platform to strengthen its real-world grounding.
The final section lacks a cohesive conclusion tying together insights from all three objects. Add a summary that reflects on the IA prompt holistically.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content:

three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of

than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
